testcase.name: SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › Login Form SQL Injection › should prevent SQL injection in login forms
SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › Login Form SQL Injection › should prevent time-based blind SQL injection
SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › Search Functionality SQL Injection › should prevent SQL injection in search queries
SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › API Parameter SQL Injection › should prevent SQL injection in API query parameters
SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › API Parameter SQL Injection › should prevent SQL injection in API path parameters
SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › Header-based SQL Injection › should prevent SQL injection through headers
SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › Cookie-based SQL Injection › should prevent SQL injection through cookies
SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › JSON Body SQL Injection › should prevent SQL injection in JSON request bodies
SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › JSON Body SQL Injection › should prevent second-order SQL injection
SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › Vulnerability Scanning › should pass comprehensive SQL injection vulnerability scan
SS-01: SQL Injection Prevention › Parameterized Query Validation › should use parameterized queries for all database operations
SS-02: XSS Protection › Reflected XSS Prevention › should prevent reflected XSS in search functionality
SS-02: XSS Protection › Reflected XSS Prevention › should prevent reflected XSS in URL parameters
SS-02: XSS Protection › Stored XSS Prevention › should prevent stored XSS in prompt content
SS-02: XSS Protection › Stored XSS Prevention › should prevent stored XSS in user profile fields
SS-02: XSS Protection › DOM-based XSS Prevention › should prevent DOM XSS through hash fragments
SS-02: XSS Protection › DOM-based XSS Prevention › should prevent DOM XSS through client-side templates
SS-02: XSS Protection › Content Security Policy › should have proper CSP headers
SS-02: XSS Protection › Content Security Policy › should prevent inline script execution without nonce
SS-02: XSS Protection › SVG XSS Prevention › should prevent XSS through SVG uploads
SS-02: XSS Protection › JSON Response XSS Prevention › should prevent XSS in JSON responses
SS-02: XSS Protection › Output Encoding Validation › should properly encode output in different contexts
SS-02: XSS Protection › Comprehensive XSS Scan › should pass comprehensive XSS vulnerability scan
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Complexity Requirements › should enforce minimum password length
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Complexity Requirements › should require mixed case, numbers, and special characters
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Complexity Requirements › should reject commonly used passwords
SS-03: Authentication Security › Account Lockout Mechanism › should lock account after multiple failed login attempts
SS-03: Authentication Security › Account Lockout Mechanism › should reset failed attempts on successful login
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Reset Security › should generate secure, unpredictable reset tokens
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Reset Security › should expire reset tokens after a reasonable time
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Reset Security › should invalidate tokens after single use
SS-03: Authentication Security › Multi-Factor Authentication › should support MFA setup and verification
SS-03: Authentication Security › Timing Attack Prevention › should have consistent response times for valid/invalid users
SS-03: Authentication Security › Session Security › should invalidate sessions on password change
SS-03: Authentication Security › Session Security › should prevent concurrent session hijacking
SS-03: Authentication Security › Authentication Token Security › should use secure JWT configuration
SS-03: Authentication Security › Authentication Token Security › should validate token signature
SS-03: Authentication Security › Brute Force Protection › should rate limit login attempts
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Storage Security › should never expose password hashes
SS-04: Session Management › Session Token Randomness › should generate cryptographically secure session tokens
SS-04: Session Management › Session Token Randomness › should not use predictable session identifiers
SS-04: Session Management › Session Expiration › should expire sessions after inactivity
SS-04: Session Management › Session Expiration › should properly handle session timeout warnings
SS-04: Session Management › Logout Functionality › should properly invalidate sessions on logout
SS-04: Session Management › Logout Functionality › should clear all session data on logout
SS-04: Session Management › Concurrent Sessions › should handle concurrent sessions securely
SS-04: Session Management › CSRF Protection › should include CSRF tokens in state-changing requests
SS-04: Session Management › CSRF Protection › should reject cross-origin requests without proper CSRF token
SS-04: Session Management › CSRF Protection › should validate referer header for state-changing requests
SS-04: Session Management › Session Fixation Prevention › should regenerate session ID on login
SS-04: Session Management › Session Fixation Prevention › should not accept externally provided session IDs
SS-04: Session Management › Session Storage Security › should use secure cookie attributes
SS-04: Session Management › Session Storage Security › should not store sensitive data in localStorage
SS-04: Session Management › Session Hijacking Prevention › should detect and prevent session hijacking attempts
SS-05: Data Encryption › HTTPS Enforcement › should redirect HTTP to HTTPS in production
SS-05: Data Encryption › HTTPS Enforcement › should include HSTS header with proper configuration
SS-05: Data Encryption › TLS Configuration › should use strong TLS version
SS-05: Data Encryption › TLS Configuration › should not expose server version information
SS-05: Data Encryption › Secure Cookie Configuration › should set secure cookie attributes
SS-05: Data Encryption › Secure Cookie Configuration › should not transmit cookies over unencrypted connections
SS-05: Data Encryption › Data at Rest Encryption › should not expose sensitive data in API responses
SS-05: Data Encryption › Data at Rest Encryption › should encrypt sensitive user data
SS-05: Data Encryption › API Communication Security › should use secure headers for API requests
SS-05: Data Encryption › API Communication Security › should not cache sensitive responses
SS-05: Data Encryption › Password Storage Security › should use strong password hashing
SS-05: Data Encryption › Password Storage Security › should not allow password reuse
SS-05: Data Encryption › Sensitive Data Transmission › should not include sensitive data in URLs
SS-05: Data Encryption › Sensitive Data Transmission › should use POST for sensitive operations
SS-05: Data Encryption › Cryptographic Security › should use secure random number generation
SS-05: Data Encryption › Comprehensive Encryption Scan › should pass comprehensive encryption vulnerability scan

SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Complexity Requirements › should require mixed case, numbers, and special characters

SS-03: Authentication Security › Account Lockout Mechanism › should reset failed attempts on successful login

SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Reset Security › should expire reset tokens after a reasonable time

SS-03: Authentication Security › Multi-Factor Authentication › should support MFA setup and verification

SS-03: Authentication Security › Session Security › should invalidate sessions on password change

SS-04: Session Management › Session Token Randomness › should generate cryptographically secure session tokens
SS-04: Session Management › Session Token Randomness › should not use predictable session identifiers
SS-04: Session Management › Session Expiration › should expire sessions after inactivity
SS-04: Session Management › Session Expiration › should properly handle session timeout warnings
SS-04: Session Management › Logout Functionality › should properly invalidate sessions on logout
SS-04: Session Management › Logout Functionality › should clear all session data on logout
SS-04: Session Management › Concurrent Sessions › should handle concurrent sessions securely
SS-04: Session Management › CSRF Protection › should include CSRF tokens in state-changing requests
SS-04: Session Management › CSRF Protection › should reject cross-origin requests without proper CSRF token
SS-04: Session Management › CSRF Protection › should validate referer header for state-changing requests
SS-04: Session Management › Session Fixation Prevention › should regenerate session ID on login

SS-04: Session Management › Session Storage Security › should not store sensitive data in localStorage

SS-05: Data Encryption › HTTPS Enforcement › should include HSTS header with proper configuration
SS-05: Data Encryption › TLS Configuration › should use strong TLS version
SS-05: Data Encryption › TLS Configuration › should not expose server version information
SS-05: Data Encryption › Secure Cookie Configuration › should set secure cookie attributes
SS-05: Data Encryption › Secure Cookie Configuration › should not transmit cookies over unencrypted connections
SS-05: Data Encryption › Data at Rest Encryption › should not expose sensitive data in API responses
SS-05: Data Encryption › Data at Rest Encryption › should encrypt sensitive user data
SS-05: Data Encryption › API Communication Security › should use secure headers for API requests
SS-05: Data Encryption › API Communication Security › should not cache sensitive responses
SS-05: Data Encryption › Password Storage Security › should use strong password hashing
SS-05: Data Encryption › Password Storage Security › should not allow password reuse
SS-05: Data Encryption › Sensitive Data Transmission › should not include sensitive data in URLs
SS-05: Data Encryption › Sensitive Data Transmission › should use POST for sensitive operations

SS-02: XSS Protection › Output Encoding Validation › should properly encode output in different contexts

SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Complexity Requirements › should enforce minimum password length
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Complexity Requirements › should require mixed case, numbers, and special characters
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Complexity Requirements › should reject commonly used passwords
SS-03: Authentication Security › Account Lockout Mechanism › should lock account after multiple failed login attempts
SS-03: Authentication Security › Account Lockout Mechanism › should reset failed attempts on successful login
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Reset Security › should generate secure, unpredictable reset tokens
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Reset Security › should expire reset tokens after a reasonable time
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Reset Security › should invalidate tokens after single use
SS-03: Authentication Security › Multi-Factor Authentication › should support MFA setup and verification
SS-03: Authentication Security › Timing Attack Prevention › should have consistent response times for valid/invalid users
SS-03: Authentication Security › Session Security › should invalidate sessions on password change
SS-03: Authentication Security › Session Security › should prevent concurrent session hijacking
SS-03: Authentication Security › Authentication Token Security › should use secure JWT configuration
SS-03: Authentication Security › Authentication Token Security › should validate token signature
SS-03: Authentication Security › Brute Force Protection › should rate limit login attempts
SS-03: Authentication Security › Password Storage Security › should never expose password hashes
SS-04: Session Management › Session Token Randomness › should generate cryptographically secure session tokens
SS-04: Session Management › Session Token Randomness › should not use predictable session identifiers
SS-04: Session Management › Session Expiration › should expire sessions after inactivity
SS-04: Session Management › Session Expiration › should properly handle session timeout warnings

SS-04: Session Management › Logout Functionality › should clear all session data on logout
SS-04: Session Management › Concurrent Sessions › should handle concurrent sessions securely
SS-04: Session Management › CSRF Protection › should include CSRF tokens in state-changing requests
SS-04: Session Management › CSRF Protection › should reject cross-origin requests without proper CSRF token
SS-04: Session Management › CSRF Protection › should validate referer header for state-changing requests
SS-04: Session Management › Session Fixation Prevention › should regenerate session ID on login
SS-04: Session Management › Session Fixation Prevention › should not accept externally provided session IDs
SS-04: Session Management › Session Storage Security › should use secure cookie attributes
SS-04: Session Management › Session Storage Security › should not store sensitive data in localStorage
SS-04: Session Management › Session Hijacking Prevention › should detect and prevent session hijacking attempts

SS-05: Data Encryption › HTTPS Enforcement › should include HSTS header with proper configuration

SS-05: Data Encryption › Secure Cookie Configuration › should not transmit cookies over unencrypted connections

SS-05: Data Encryption › Data at Rest Encryption › should encrypt sensitive user data

SS-05: Data Encryption › API Communication Security › should not cache sensitive responses
